observations.recommendation:
Recommendation

We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer to
submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every year as mandated by law, and
to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant; and to
create an inventory committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all
properties of the LGU.

The audit team leader suggested that the LGU should have an inventory
committee per Office to facilitate the inventory taking and the assigned General Service
Officer to consolidate the report and conduct of ocular inspection to validate the
inventory. For Public Infrastructures the Local Chief Executive mentioned that they will
start and submit the inventory report.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management observe the general guidelines stipulated in
COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of
Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote Good
Governance through transparency and accountability.

Recommendation

We recommended that:
a. The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement
vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the
Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise,
we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other
emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the
Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445);
b. Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize
work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload
and meet deadlines on reports submission; and

Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing
Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the
same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time
prescribed by the regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management should require the municipal accountant and
municipal treasurer to observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts, in
accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006, so that the Auditor could
perform timely audit of the financial transactions of the agency.

Recommendation:
We recommended that officials concerned particularly the Acting General Services
Officer and its local Bids and Award Committee personnel to regularly and to adhere to
proper internal processes by providing the resident auditor in advance with copies of the
Purchase Orders and within 24 hours after acceptance of such procurement inform the
assigned audit team leader of the deliveries made.

Recommendation:
The management shall prioritize registration with PhilGEPS not only to take
advantage of its built-in efficiencies but also avail of volume discounts inherent in bulk
purchasing. It may also promote transparency and efficiency in the procurement process
and give all interested parties an equal opportunity to bid.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following:
a. Compliance with pertinent laws and regulations on disbursements shall be strictly
observed. Completeness of supporting documents is necessary to establish correctness
and validity of the claim for payment.
b. The accounting office being the Internal Control Unit and in-charge in the processing of
money claims of the agency shall review and check well the audit.
c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer failed to reconcile the balances
in the related accounts in the books of the Agency and the cashbook balance of the
Municipal Treasurer on quarterly basis leading to balance discrepancies due to timing
differences in the recording of transactions.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit
regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us to validate and verify the
transactions.

Recommendation

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of law as
cited above by remitting promptly LGUs share and employee’s personal contributions
and deduction in order not to hamper the benefits that the employee may receive and to
avoid penalty and legal sanctions and past due accounts like this should be given priority
to determine the correct unremitted balances, so that, said account will no longer be
carried over and over again in the financial statement.

Recommendation

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of tax law as
cited above by withholding and remitting promptly suppliers deduction in order not to
render legal sanctions.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant; and to create an inventory committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended creation of the Municipal Inter-Agency Task Force COVID- 19 with its functions and mandate to strictly implement the approved policies and regulations of the national IATF – COVID 19 and strongly imposed its own localize guideline restrictions, medical protocols of incoming and out-going various travellers and to cushion the severe impact of eventual super-spreader and rise in COVID-19 infections among its constituents. Providing Quarantine/Isolation facilities to address and confine suspected cases of COVID-19 infected individuals. Immediate distribution of cash and in-kind assistance to its affected constituent during imposed community-wide lockdown to alleviate them from such difficult situation of making a living and putting foods on their tables. And such other similar activities to stop the spread and community transmission of such deadly COVID-19 threat.
observations.managementAction: management Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials of the Municipality of Jolo to support all claims with sufficient and required documentations, particularly, on the utilization of the COVID 19 appropriated funds in their procurement of medical related goods and services. Likewise, management must strictly observe laws, rules and regulations as provided for in Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on proper and sufficient required documentations and Section 52.1 of RA 9184 on Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement.
observations.managementAction: management Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management direct the concerned accountant to observe the perpetual system in recording purchases and to use the RSMI in recording issuances and consumption of stocks and prepare adjusting entries for all noted errors in recording to correct the balances of inventory accounts.
observations.managementAction: management Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended control over supplies or property be adopted and it demands that, proper and accurate recording as to its valuation, existence, ownership and completeness shall be kept for all incoming and out- going supplies or property in pursuant to Sec. 42, 45 to 46 of NGAS Manual Vol. I
observations.managementAction: management Implemented

Section 4(114) (E2) “Punishable acts or omissions – every officer or
employee of the government of the Philippines or any of its agencies and
instrumentalities, its political subdivision, xxx charged with the duty to deduct
and withhold any internal revenue tax and to remit the same in accordance with
these regulations shall, upon conviction of each act or omission herein – below
specified, be fined in a sum of not less than five thousand pesos (P5,000.00) but
not more than Fifty Thousand Pesos (P50,000.00) or imprisoned for a term of not
less than six (6) months and one (1) but not more than (2) years or both.”

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposits her collection
regularly as prescribed and to adopt necessary control measures in the handling
deposits of cash collections so as not to expose the scarce government monies to
the risk of loss and misuse by not depositing it intact to government depository
banks as prescribed.

We recommended that management makes a representation with
Municipal Accountant for the timely preparation of the BRS.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal General
Services Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE
every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and
reconcile the records with the Accountant; and to create an inventory committee
that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the
LGU.

We have recommended that accountable officers must strictly adhere to
the COA guidelines that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any
officials or employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper
accounting thereof is made. Furthermore, liquidation of cash advances should be
made as required by regulation.

We recommend, that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and
Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office
as required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of
the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommend the management should instruct concerned officials to
submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the
deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing
month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries
and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the
requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section
122(2),P.D 1445);

Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to
maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work
overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and

Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions
and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting
Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

We recommend that all Revenue Collectors of the Municipality of Jolo
be directed to submit the RAAF at the end of each month to the Audit Team
through the Municipal Accounting Office.

We recommend that:
Management observe the general guidelines stipulated in COA Cir. No.
2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs,
Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is to promote Good
Governance through transparency and accountability.

The management shall see to it that all developmental projects were duly
verified/inspected by the authorized COA officials.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and
change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid
Waste Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an
arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in
order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results,
and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of law
as cited above by remitting promptly LGUs share and employee’s personal
contributions and deduction in order not to hamper the benefits that the employee
may receive and to avoid penalty and legal sanctions and past due accounts like
this should be given priority to determine the correct unremitted balances, so that,
said account will no longer be carried over and over again in the financial
statement.

We recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan of Jolo enact a pertinent Ordinance to enable and authorize the municipal Local Chief Executive to enter into a short and/or long term lease with potential local business individuals and/or business entities for the use of its various multi- purpose buildings and market places to help in generating more revenues and to greatly augment the needed financial resources of the municipality to achieve a genuine autonomy and be a self- reliant community

observations.recommendation: We hereby recommend the following: That the Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the regulations on procurement through shopping. Procurement above the threshold should be done thru procurement service or public bidding pursuant to the provisions under RA 9184 to foster transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most advantageous to the municipality. However, splitting by dividing the projects into artificial phases to circumvent the regulations should not be resorted to.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We recommended that management makes a representation with
Municipal Accountant for the timely preparation of the BRS.

Recommendation
We recommend that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer
to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every year as mandated
by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the
Accountant.

We hereby recommend the following:
That the Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the regulations on
procurement through shopping. Procurement above the threshold should be done thru
procurement service or public bidding pursuant to the provisions under RA 9184 to foster
transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most advantageous to the
municipality. However, splitting by dividing the projects into artificial phases to
circumvent the regulations should not be resorted to.
Stop the payment of purchases out of reimbursement basis unless circumstances
warrant its application.
Required documents on procurement shall be complete, properly numbered,
signed and dated.

Recommendation
We recommend, that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance
and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required
under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS
Manual, Volume I.

Recommendation
We recommend the management should instruct concerned officials to submit
disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set
by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise,
we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments
of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on
Audit are being complied with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445);
Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize
work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and
meet deadlines on reports submission; and
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to
submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section
within the time prescribed by the regulations.

Recommendation
We recommend that all Revenue Collectors of the Municipality of Jolo be
directed to submit the RAAF at the end of each month to the Audit Team through the
Municipal Accounting Office.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Municipal Accounting Office be directed to maintain an
updated subsidiary ledger of each collecting officers where collections are recorded upon
receipt of the daily recording for easy validation of daily collections.

We recommend that every officer of any
government agency whose duties permit or require the possession or
custody of government funds or property shall be accountable therefore
and for the safekeeping thereof in conformity with law.
Every officer of the local government unit whose duties permit or require
the possession or custody of local funds shall be properly bonded, and
such officer shall be accountable for said funds and for the safekeeping
thereof in conformity with the provisions of law.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit
regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us to validate and verify the
transactions.

We recommend that the:
Concerned officials should fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered
by these fund transfers in order to accomplish the purpose for which the funds
were granted and deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries;
and require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the liquidation reports to the
source agency.

Recommendation
We recommend that:
Management observe the general guidelines stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004
dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and
Activities of government agencies. This is to promote Good Governance through
transparency and accountability.

Recommendation
The management shall see to it that all developmental projects were duly
verified/inspected by the authorized COA officials.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change
management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste
Management Plan. Develop an Action/Implementation Plan. Draw an arrangement for
the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate
proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish
accountability of the responsible person.

We recommend that the
Sangguniang Bayan of
Jolo enact a pertinent
Ordinance to enable and
authorize the municipal
Local Chief Executive to
enter into a short and/or
long term lease with
potential local business
individuals and/or
business entities for the
use of its various multi-
purpose buildings and
market places to help in
generating more
revenues and to greatly
augment the needed
financial resources of the
municipality to achieve a
genuine autonomy and be
a self-reliant community.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan of Jolo enact a pertinent Ordinance to enable and authorize the municipal Local Chief Executive to enter into a short and/or long term lease with potential local business individuals and/or business entities for the use of its various multi-purpose buildings and market places to help in generating more revenues and to greatly augment the needed financial resources of the municipality to achieve a genuine autonomy and be a self-reliant community.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant.

We recommend that the Supply Officer must conduct an official inventory taking in order to report the actual properties that are unserviceable from serviceable properties and prepare the necessary reclassification of accounts and adjustments to establish the correctness of the Financial Statement of the agency as a whole.
However, if found to be valueless or unsalable, it may be destroyed in their presence and if found to be valuable, it may be sold at public auction to the highest bidder in accordance with section 502 and 503 of the GAAM Vol. I. and Section 79 of PD 1445. Create a Disposal Committee who shall recommend the proper mode of disposal and require the Supply Officer to immediately submit to the Disposal Committee through their respective heads the inventory and inspection of unserviceable property and waste materials opt for sale.

We hereby recommend  the following:
That the Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the regulations on procurement through shopping. Procurement above the threshold should be done thru procurement service or public bidding pursuant to the provisions under RA 9184 to foster transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most advantageous to the municipality. However, splitting by dividing the projects into artificial phases to circumvent the regulations should not be resorted to.
Stop the payment of purchases out of reimbursement basis unless circumstances warrant its application.
Required documents on procurement shall be complete, properly numbered, signed and dated.

We recommend,  that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
We recommend that all Revenue Collectors of the Municipality of Jolo be directed to submit the RAAF at the end of each month to the Audit Team through the Municipal Accounting Office.

We recommend that the Municipal Accounting Office be directed to maintain an updated subsidiary ledger of each collecting officers where collections are recorded upon receipt of the daily REC for easy validation of daily collections.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us to validate and verify the transactions.

We recommend that the:
Concerned officials should fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered by these fund transfers in order to accomplish the purpose for which the funds were granted and deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries; and require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the liquidation reports to the source agency.

We recommend  that:
Management observe the general guidelines stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies.  This is to promote Good Governance through transparency and accountability.
The management shall see to it that all developmental projects were duly verified/inspected by the authorized COA officials.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan.  Develop an Action/Implementation Plan.  Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all withheld mandatory deductions such as GSIS, HDMF, and PhilHealth contributions, and BIR tax withheld should be remitted immediately to the agency concerned, otherwise, corresponding punitive measures should be imposed upon those who repeatedly violate the provisions of law.

Management should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contribution should be answerable for their actions.
observations.managementAction: The Management has not been complying with this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant.

We hereby recommended the following:
That the Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the regulations on procurement through shopping. Procurement above the threshold should be done thru procurement service or public bidding pursuant to the provisions under RA 9184 to foster transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most advantageous to the municipality. However, splitting by dividing the projects into artificial phases to circumvent the regulations should not be resorted to.
Stop the payment of purchases out of reimbursement basis unless circumstances warrant its application.
Required documents on procurement shall be complete, properly numbered, signed and dated.

We recommended  that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that:
The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2),P.D. 1445);
Instructions shall also be given to the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission; and
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit regularly and on time all required reports needed in order for us to validate and verify the transactions.

We recommended that the:
Concerned officials should  fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered by these fund transfers in order to accomplish the purpose for which the funds were granted and deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the liquidation reports to the source agency.
We recommended that the concerned officials shall submit to the Audit Team/Auditor the documents for validation and verification.

We recommended that:
Management observe the general guidelines stipulated in COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies.  This is to promote Good Governance through transparency and accountability.
The management shall see to it that all developmental projects were duly verified/inspected by the authorized COA officials.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management be formalize and change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan.  Develop an Action/Implementation Plan.  Draw an arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner in order to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability of the responsible person.

It is recommended that LGU must submit and furnished copies of contract and memorandum of agreement and their required attachment within five (5) days from their issuances to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management noted the herein audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.

Efforts also had been made by the responsible officials concerned in addressing the issue of inventory taking.

The inventory committee had started their conduct of inventory and was able to submit late their PPE inventory.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that municipal accountant and the municipal treasurer be instructed to strictly adhere the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial statements and disbursement vouchers and other accounting reports. Instruct the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission. 

Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.
